limitations: This stage did not patch or import FL17 artifacts.
Current canonical extracted root /mnt/data/Metablooms_OS was not present; ZIP/receipt artifacts were used as authority evidence.
research_question: Does the FL17 compare/merge-plan workflow remain valid after correcting the skipped SEE/CE governance preflight?

source_index.claim: AI governance should manage risks through deliberate design/development/use/evaluation practices and maintain trustworthiness considerations.
ChatGPT UI/tool/project capabilities change over time, so platform-dependent workflow planning requires current source checks.
Progress, success, waiting, and error information should be explicit status information rather than hidden context changes.
Architecture descriptions should include views governed by viewpoints, correspondence/consistency rules, known issues, and evaluation methods.

validated_claims.claim: MetaBlooms governance should not be optional or selectively applied; governance gates should be mapped, measured, managed, and recorded as part of risk management.
Because ChatGPT UI/tool/project behavior changes, MetaBlooms stages that depend on ChatGPT behavior should include a current-source preflight or explicit bounded exception.
Inline tracker status changes are a status-message problem and should remain explicit, visible, and evidence-bound.
The OS architecture and merge plan should document views/concerns, known issues, consistency rules, and evaluation methods rather than relying on informal judgement.
